qa_pairs.question: What will be the default standard environment for all clusters after April 1, 2024, and how can a user specify a different default environment?
qa_pairs.answer: After April 1, 2024, `StdEnv/2023` will be the default environment for all clusters. Users can specify their own default environment at any time by modifying the `$HOME/.modulerc` file. For instance, running `echo "module-version StdEnv/2020 default" >> $HOME/.modulerc` will set the default to `StdEnv/2020`. Users must log out and log in again for this change to take effect.

qa_pairs.question: When the `StdEnv` changes, do users need to reinstall or recompile their code, and what is the support status of earlier standard environments?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you compile your own code or have installed R or Python packages, you should recompile your code or reinstall the necessary packages with the newest version of the standard environment. While earlier environments and their dependent software will remain available, versions `2016.4` and `2018.3` are no longer supported, and it is recommended not to use them. Staff will only install software in the new `StdEnv/2023` environment.

qa_pairs.question: Can modules from different standard environments be mixed within a single job on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, it is recommended to use a single standard environment for a given job. Attempting to mix modules from different environments within a single job can lead to unpredictable results and errors. Different jobs, however, can use different standard environments by explicitly loading one at the beginning of each job.
